evidence: Among 188 screened family members, 94 were phenotypically affected, including 85 with genotype confirmation of the D1790G SCN5A mutation and 9 non-genotyped obligate carriers. evidence: Sixteen study participants carried R176W and, as a group, had a 22.2 ms longer adjusted QT interval than non-carriers. The paper states that personal or family arrhythmia symptoms were not systematically ascertained, so individual affected and unaffected counts are indeterminate.
source_location: Results, second and fourth paragraphs

evidence: K897T occurred in five LQTS screening probands and an unspecified several relatives. In the separate KCNQ1-Fin cohort, Table 2 shows 63 K897T heterozygotes and 17 T897T homozygotes (80 variant-positive patients), with genotype-specific QTc values. The paper does not establish the overlap or give an exact count for the additional family carriers.
source_location: Results, 'Novel polymorphism in the HERG gene,' pp. 3-4; Table 2, p. 4

evidence: The mutation was found in 40 heterozygous individuals, while the two affected daughters were homozygous. Of 35 clinically classified heterozygotes, 10 were symptomatic and 25 asymptomatic. Both homozygous siblings had markedly prolonged QTc and early ventricular arrhythmias.
source_location: Results, 'Identification of the HERG-Fin mutation' and 'Clinical characteristics of the HERG-Fin mutation carriers'; Results, 'Phenotypic characteristics of the index family'

evidence: There were 79 molecularly ascertained R518* genotype-positive individuals; ECGs were available and their group mean QTc was 462 ± 34 ms. No individual affected/unaffected classification was reported.
source_location: Methods, study sample, PDF p.2; Results, Table 1, PDF p.4

evidence: The study states, “We identified 170 mutation-carriers of the LQTS mutation Y111C/KCNQ1 in 37 apparently unrelated Swedish index families.” It describes Y111C as having a mild/benign clinical phenotype, but gives no number of carriers with cardiac manifestations or explicitly asymptomatic/clinically normal carriers.
source_location: Main text, Results, opening paragraph; Abstract, Background and Methods; Introduction, second paragraph

evidence: The South African founder population included 168 mutation carriers carrying the identical-by-descent KCNQ1 p.Ala341Val mutation. No exact division of these carriers into affected and explicitly unaffected people is supplied.
source_location: Abstract, Methods and Results; study-flow figure (genotyping successful: MC=168)

evidence: Among 451 descendants in the Finnish G589D subgroup, 243 were carriers. However, the subgroup also comprised 191 nuclear families with carrier parents, and distinct total carriers cannot be reconstructed without double-counting. The paper states that phenotypic characteristics of G589D carriers were not included; it only cites an external report in which 30% were symptomatic.
source_location: Methods, Study Population, Genotyping, and Phenotyping; Results, paragraphs beginning 'Our study included 240 pedigrees' and 'Since most pedigrees involved unique mutations'

evidence: The study reports 60 genetically confirmed Y111C carriers, including three children younger than 1 year subsequently excluded from analysis. Among adults, one Y111C patient had aborted cardiac arrest, but the remaining symptom counts and the 23 symptomatic versus 52 asymptomatic LQT1 totals were not divided by variant.
source_location: Methods, opening study-population paragraph; Results, Clinical Characteristics; Results, VCG Analysis—Comparison of LQTS mutation carriers

evidence: The study reports 42 genetically confirmed R518X carriers. Adult symptom status was reported only for the combined Y111C/R518X LQT1 group (23 symptomatic and 52 asymptomatic), so variant-specific affected and unaffected counts cannot be determined.
source_location: Methods, opening study-population paragraph; Results, Clinical Characteristics; Results, VCG Analysis—Comparison of LQTS mutation carriers

evidence: The study identified 124 carriers among 5,521 Amish participants, with all carrier results confirmed by Sanger sequencing. Of 88 carriers undergoing detailed follow-up, 34 (38.6%) had clinical evidence of LQTS, defined as QTc >500 ms and/or Schwartz score >3.5. No deduplicated count of explicitly asymptomatic or clinically normal carriers was provided.
source_location: Main PDF, Results, 'Identification of the c.671C>T (p.T224M) variant in KCNQ1,' p.5; Results, 'Clinical characteristics of p.T224M KCNQ1 carriers,' pp.7-9; Table 1, p.21; Supplemental Table 1B

evidence: The table enumerates 179 p.G357S-positive subjects. Of these, 45 had previous syncope/dizziness and 69 had basal VA, with 23 overlapping, yielding 91 affected carriers; 87 had no previous symptoms and no positive VA, while patient 37 was indeterminate. The Abstract additionally reports the mutation in 6 SCD cases, all affected, giving 185 carriers and 97 affected overall.
source_location: Preserved clinical-characteristics table, rows Patient 1–179 and columns 'Previous symptoms' and 'VA in basal test'; Abstract, Results ('179 family members and ... 6 SCD cases').
